clinical_coherence.notes: 58M presenting with new-onset seizure (likely from HPI) and progressive neurological symptoms. MRI shows ring-enhancing right frontal mass with necrosis and surrounding edema. Vitals, PMH (HTN, DM2, hyperlipidemia), and medications are realistic and internally consistent.

tool_output_consistency.notes: MRI shows classic GBM features: ring-enhancing mass with central necrosis, restricted diffusion at rim, surrounding vasogenic edema. MR perfusion shows elevated rCBV (3.8) in enhancing rim. MR spectroscopy shows elevated choline, reduced NAA, lipid/lactate peaks. CT body negative for systemic malignancy. Biopsy confirms IDH-wildtype GBM with MGMT unmethylated, TERT promoter mutation, EGFR amplification. All concordant.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD C71.9 correct for brain GBM. Molecular markers (IDH-wildtype, TERT+, EGFR amp, MGMT unmethylated) consistent with WHO 2021 classification. Differential, critical actions, and contraindicated actions all clinically sound.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Classic ring-enhancing mass with necrosis, high rCBV, and classic spectroscopy. Straightforward GBM. Appropriate calibration.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 58M with HTN, DM2. Peak GBM incidence is 55-85 years, male predominance. Highly realistic.

educational_value.notes: Good standard GBM case with comprehensive advanced imaging (perfusion, spectroscopy) and molecular profiling. Teaches WHO 2021 classification with IDH/TERT/MGMT markers.

issues_found: Sodium 152 mEq/L is significantly elevated (hypernatremia) but not addressed in lab interpretation - should raise concern for dehydration or DI
